stories.title: Chelsea blocks data centers and big rentals for a full year
stories.dek: Chelsea city council voted to freeze all new data-center and large rental-housing proposals for 12 months. Residents near potential sites avoid sudden spikes in power demand and traffic while the city studies infrastructure limits. The pause gives neighbors time to weigh how much extra electricity and water these projects would pull from local supplies.
stories.source_title: Chelsea approves one-year moratoriums on data centers, rental communities - WBMA

stories.title: Augusta Township residents vote Aug. 4 on 300-acre data-center rezoning
stories.dek: Voters will decide whether to allow rezoning that could bring a large data-center campus to Augusta Township. Approval would open the door to hundreds of megawatts of new demand on Consumers Energy lines and possible well-water withdrawals. A no vote keeps the land in agricultural or low-density use and blocks the tax-base versus service-cost trade-off.
stories.source_title: Augusta Township voters to decide fate of data center rezoning - WEMU

stories.title: Court overturns local veto of massive AI campus, raising power and water worries
stories.dek: After a township turned down the massive AI campus, a court ruling reopened the door for the $16 billion project. Neighbors now face renewed questions about who pays for new power lines and whether their wells and roads can handle the load. The decision shifts the fight from local zoning to the courtroom.
stories.source_title: Michigan township rejected a $16 billion AI data center, then a lawsuit cleared the way - Yahoo

stories.title: West Michigan residents sue hyperscale data center over constant noise
stories.dek: Homeowners near a large data center in West Michigan filed suit claiming the facility produces unreasonable and excessive noise that disrupts daily life. The lawsuit puts the operator on notice that neighbors will not quietly accept the side effects of massive server farms. Michigan ratepayers and nearby towns will watch closely to see if courts force changes or compensation.
stories.source_title: Residents Suing Hyperscale Data Center In West Michigan Over 'unreasonable, Excessive Noise' Vti Stock (0tLX9RhADB) - Mshale

stories.title: Monroe County halts all new data center and battery permits for 18 months
stories.dek: The board voted to freeze applications, shielding local roads, wells and the grid from sudden large-scale builds. Residents gain time to weigh how much power and water these projects would pull from the county. The pause applies equally to data centers and battery storage, two uses that often arrive together.
stories.source_title: Monroe County board passes 18-month moratorium on data center and battery storage permits - news8000.com

stories.title: GOP chair floats ban on utility deals that shield data centers from rate hikes
stories.dek: Michigan Republican Party chair says he may back legislation stopping DTE and Consumers Energy from shifting data-center power costs onto households. The move would affect how billions in new grid upgrades get paid for across the state. Ratepayers in both utility territories would see the biggest difference if the ban passes.
stories.source_title: Michigan GOP chair says he might support ban on DTE, Consumers giving - Yahoo

stories.title: Tech giants agree to pay for power infrastructure as Michigan shields ratepayers
stories.dek: The agreement aims to prevent ratepayers from footing the full bill for new transmission lines and upgrades. Michigan officials secured commitments from major tech companies amid rising power demand. The deal could shape how future projects are funded across the state.
stories.source_title: Michigan secures Big Tech pledge on data-center grid costs - TechInformed

stories.title: Township removes ceiling on incentives for power-hungry server farm
stories.dek: After earlier resistance, Saline Township dropped its proposed limit on how large a tax break a data-center project can receive. The move opens the door to bigger incentives for a project that could draw hundreds of megawatts and reshape local tax rolls. Residents 30 minutes away will feel the impact through school funding, road wear, and water demand tied to the campus.
stories.source_title: Data center news: Saline Township reverses course, drops cap on data center tax break - Planet Detroit

stories.title: Michigan township battles state data center law as Whitmer rolls out new pledge
stories.dek: A local township is pushing back against state rules that limit its ability to block data centers. Residents face potential noise, power strain and water use from projects that could arrive despite local opposition. Whitmer's pledge aims to speed approvals, raising stakes for communities that want more say over where these facilities land.
stories.source_title: "Fed up" Michigan township fights against state law as Whitmer unveils data center pledge - WWMT

stories.title: Utility allies slam Michigan data center critic
stories.dek: Groups linked to Consumers Energy are going after a vocal opponent of large data center projects. The attacks come as utilities face scrutiny over rising power costs tied to new server farms. Michigan residents could see higher electric bills if the projects move forward unchecked.
stories.source_title: Groups tied to Consumers Energy target Michigan data center critic - The Detroit News

stories.title: Negaunee sets Aug 13 hearing on data center zoning rules
stories.dek: Marquette County residents will weigh in next month on proposed rules that could shape where and how large data centers locate. The ordinance directly affects land use, power demand and local tax base in Negaunee. A clear policy now could limit or invite projects that alter the township's character and bills.
stories.source_title: Public hearing for Negaunee data center ordinance to be held on Aug. 13 - The Mining Journal -

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Discussion of draft data center ordinance; no projects proposed or approved yet

meetings.body: Charter Township of Oxford Planning Commission
meetings.topic: Discussion of draft data center ordinance; no action expected, public input sought on zoning rules for potential future projects
